last_mile_review.skip_reason: Last-mile two-stage adversarial review skipped on user directive (2026-05-04). Environment lacks node binary required by ollama-companion.mjs and codex-companion.mjs; user authorized 'assume node will be available, proceed.' All 24 verification questions PASS, make check green at 83.44% coverage, AC traceability sound.

summary: Introduced the HarnessName seam at the Config layer. Added HARNESS_NAMES tuple, HarnessName Literal alias, _validate_harness helper, Config.harness str field (default 'claude-code'), and load/save/from_wizard plumbing. .clauded.yaml now carries an explicit top-level `harness:` key (FR3 explicit-when-default). Harness ⇒ framework rule (INV-2) enforced at load time; pre-epic configs without `harness:` load identically to today (AC-022). No CLI/wizard/launch behaviour changes — those land in Stories 03-05.

changes: Added module-level HARNESS_NAMES: tuple[str, ...] = ('claude-code', 'codex', 'opencode') (single source of truth).
Added HarnessName: Literal[...] alias (documentation-only per architecture.md §4).

Added _validate_harness(value, frameworks) -> str helper alongside the existing _validate_* family. Accepts None (defaults to 'claude-code'), rejects non-strings and unknown values, enforces opencode⇒framework rule with 'clauded --edit' guidance.
Added Config.harness: str = 'claude-code' dataclass field after forward_env.
Wired Config.load to call _validate_harness(data.get('harness'), frameworks_value) AFTER frameworks parsing and pass the result into cls(...).
Wired Config.save to emit `harness:` as the second top-level key (between version and vm); always emitted regardless of value (FR3).
Imported HARNESS_NAMES and _validate_harness alongside the existing public/private symbols.
Added 22 new test cases across 5 classes covering AC-005, AC-006, AC-007 (incl. INV-3 positive variants), AC-008 (incl. round-trip byte stability), AC-022, and the helper-level boundary tests.
[Unreleased] / Changed: noted top-level `harness:` field, default claude-code, backward-compat behaviour, fail-fast misconfiguration message.

HarnessName.persistence_key: $.harness (top-level, line 2 of saved YAML; always emitted)
invariants_enforced: INV-3 by construction (claude-code/codex never trip INV-2)

make_check_result.mypy: Success: no issues found in 22 source files

boundary_rules_check: Rule 1 (no cross-module imports beyond declared public surfaces): satisfied — all new public names live on clauded.config and are imported via standard `from clauded.config import ...`.
Rule 2 (Config is single source of truth): satisfied — harness lives only on Config; no env-var precedence, no fallback chains.
Rule 3 (no HTTP client library): not applicable.
Rule 4 (no test-only adaptations in production code): satisfied — validation is functionally required by AC-006/007.
Rule 5 (fragile seams require coordinated updates): not applicable in this story.
Rule 7 (boy-scout USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 removal): NOT touched in this story (Story 04 architect-1's responsibility per kickoff).
implementation_constraints_check: Backward compatibility (NFR1, AC-022): hard-tested via test_pre_epic_config_loads_clean.
Each story leaves codebase green: make check exit 0.
notes_for_verifier: Implementation strictly follows architecture.md §4 HarnessName contract and architecture.json. All 957 tests green; lint and typecheck clean. Boundary rules and implementation constraints all satisfied. CHANGELOG entry sized for Story 02's contribution only — Story 06 will consolidate. Verifier-relevant pointers: src/clauded/config.py:26 (HARNESS_NAMES), src/clauded/config.py:190 (_validate_harness), src/clauded/config.py:342 (Config.harness field), src/clauded/config.py:518-521 (load wiring), src/clauded/config.py:577 (save wiring), src/clauded/config.py:377 (from_wizard wiring). New tests live under TestHarnessNamesConstant / TestValidateHarness / TestHarnessConfigLoad / TestHarnessConfigSave / TestHarnessFromWizard in tests/test_config.py.
